behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Agentic, because failure can come from real vision-model judgment on explicit content. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration is too low because stubbed classifier output would always satisfy the rule and miss real misclassification; Workflow is too high because this behavior hinges on one model decision plus deterministic hold logic, not multiple agentic decisions. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run against the real vision moderation model with a labeled explicit-image set; submit posts through moderation and assert each is flagged, routed to human-review hold state, and never reaches published state.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Agentic, since correctness depends on real text-model robustness to obfuscation patterns. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration is too low because mocked outputs hide whether the model actually catches substitutions/lookalikes; Workflow is too high because this is a single model-quality behavior, not a multi-agentic chain. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real text moderation model and a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants (leet, homoglyphs, spacing tricks); assert they are flagged and held for review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Integration, because the failure boundary is module handoff/orchestration (image result + text result -> publish state). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit is too low because this is cross-module state propagation; System is disqualified by behavior text ('passes both content checks') which makes model outputs a precondition and does not require real OS/network behavior; Agentic is disqualified because this behavior does not evaluate model output quality, only routing after pass results. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service + state/store + publisher in-process; inject deterministic 'pass' outputs from both classifiers and assert the post reaches published state with no hold record and no drop.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Workflow, because this explicitly chains multiple agentic stages (image model then text model) plus final decision in one end-to-end SLA. PASS2-AUDIT: Agentic (single-step) is too low because behavior text requires the full sequence; System alone is too low because it can validate infra latency but not the multi-agentic pipeline boundary. FINAL: Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through real image and text model calls in sequence, measure submit-to-final-decision latency, and assert p95 completion is under 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: System, because real failure often appears at external API/HTTP timeout and service-unavailable boundaries. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration could catch basic retry branching with stubs, but MOCK-HIDE applies: stubs can miss real timeout/error-shape behavior that leads to accidental auto-approve; Agentic is disqualified by behavior text since this tests availability handling, not model-output correctness. FINAL: System.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real unavailability conditions for the vision dependency (e.g., connection refusal/timeout/503 from a test endpoint) and assert image posts enter retry queue with no auto-approval path taken.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Agentic, because consistency for borderline content depends on real model behavior across repeated calls. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration is too low because fixed mocks cannot expose model variance; Workflow is too high because this is repeated evaluation of one agentic decision point, not a multi-stage agent pipeline. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly to the real vision moderation model within a defined short window and assert decision consistency rate meets threshold (or deterministic cache policy is honored if configured).
